name: South Sudan
headline: South Sudan FLOOR CONFIRMATION at 0.0 / Critical — UN Emergency Relief Coordinator Tom Fletcher warned Security Council of risk of 'full-scale famine and collapse' (April 18 UNSC briefing). HRW May 6 report 'Civilians starving in conflict areas' is new primary-source documentation. 10 million people require humanitarian assistance; 267,000+ displaced in Jonglei state alone in 2026. US aid cuts forcing scale-back across UN agencies and INGOs. Fangak hospital bombardment (May 3: 7 killed, 20 injured) pending full IHL-protected-site documentation. Three compounding categories this cycle: (a) famine-trajectory escalation; (b) hospital-strike conduct (IHL-protected site); (c) aid-withdrawal-induced EMP compounding (US aid cut downstream impact). Floor sustained at 0.0; conduct documentation updated.

AWR.rationale: Floor sustained. State-level recognition of famine trajectory absent or inadequate; international monitoring (UN ERC Fletcher) producing primary documentation.

EMP.rationale: Floor sustained. 10M needing assistance, 267K displaced in Jonglei alone in 2026 — scale exceeds any state-level empathic engagement capacity demonstrated.

ACT.rationale: Floor sustained. AC4 resource mobilization: domestic capacity inadequate; international resource mobilization disrupted by US aid cuts. Fangak hospital bombardment indicates conduct in opposite direction of need.

EQU.rationale: Floor sustained. EQ4 inclusion: inter-communal violence facilitated by political elites continues (canonical floor designation evidence).

BND.rationale: Floor sustained. B3 scope: Fangak hospital strike is IHL-protected-site conduct event; if confirmed, this is a war-crime-category event.

ACC.rationale: Floor sustained. AB4 reparation: zero accountability mechanism for state-tolerated inter-communal violence (canonical floor designation evidence). UN Commission on Human Rights in South Sudan continues documentation.

SYS.rationale: Floor sustained. S1 causal reasoning: famine trajectory reflects systems-level collapse — domestic institutions cannot prevent or address it; UNMISS expired April 30 with no credible succession.

INT.rationale: Floor sustained. I1 consistency under pressure: domestic political elites continue to facilitate inter-communal violence even under UN ERC 'full-scale famine and collapse' warning.

evidence.summary: Al Jazeera April 18 2026: UN aid chief warns of possible full-scale famine in South Sudan; UN ERC Fletcher Security Council briefing.
HRW May 6 2026: civilians starving in South Sudan's conflict areas — new primary-source documentation since last assessment.
IRC overview: 10 million people need help in South Sudan; 267,000+ displaced in Jonglei state in 2026.

keyEvidence: April 18 2026: UN ERC Fletcher warns Security Council of 'full-scale famine and collapse' risk.
May 6 2026: HRW 'Civilians starving in South Sudan's conflict areas' — new primary source.
10 million people need humanitarian assistance; 267,000+ displaced in Jonglei state alone in 2026.
May 3 2026: Fangak hospital bombardment — 7 killed, 20 injured (IHL-protected site, pending full documentation).
US aid cuts forcing scale-back across UN agencies and INGOs — aid-withdrawal-induced EMP compounding.
Canonical April 30 floor designation: UNMISS mandate expiry April 30 without credible succession plan.
rationale: Floor sustained. Three new conduct categories this cycle: (1) famine-trajectory-escalation under UN ERC warning; (2) hospital-strike (Fangak, May 3) — IHL-protected-site conduct event; (3) aid-withdrawal-induced EMP compounding (US aid cut downstream impact on UN agencies/INGOs). The aid-withdrawal-induced compounding is a methodologically significant category — it documents that EMP-dimension failure can be amplified by upstream-actor decisions, even where the upstream actor is not the entity under assessment. Floor-exit criteria not in trajectory.
watches: UN Commission on Human Rights in South Sudan reporting cycle
carryForward: UNMISS expiry April 30 without credible succession
Sustained pattern of state-tolerated inter-communal violence
newConductCategoryNote: The aid-withdrawal-induced EMP compounding sub-category is methodologically novel — it isolates the upstream-actor decision (US aid cut) as a documented amplifier of EMP-dimension failure in a downstream-actor entity. Recommend methodology team consider whether upstream-actor compounding belongs in the assessed entity's record or in a cross-entity 'compounded-harm' tracking layer.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
